front.title: Long-Term, Crypto-Agile Preservation of Authorization Evidence (EP-EVIDENCE-RECORD)

abstract.t: Regulations increasingly require that records of who authorized a high-risk action be retained for years (e.g. five years under DORA, six under HIPAA and SEC 17a-4). Any fixed signature or hash algorithm used to protect such a record weakens over time; a receipt signed today with Ed25519 over SHA-256 may be cryptographically attackable before its retention period ends. This document defines EP-EVIDENCE-RECORD, an OPTIONAL profile that preserves the verifiability of EMILIA Protocol authorization receipts (and other artifacts) across algorithm aging, using a renewal chain in the style of the Evidence Record Syntax [RFC4998]. Each renewal time-attests the entire prior renewal under a fresh, stronger algorithm before the older one is broken, so an unbroken chain links the original artifact to the most recent renewal. The record is offline- verifiable, fail- closed, and maintained as cross-language conformance vectors that three independent implementations agree on.
